summary: The FUXA script engine executes arbitrary operating system commands through the unauthenticated /api/runscript endpoint in the default configuration. The exploit sends a POST request with script.test=true and code containing require('child_process').execSync() via Module._compile, achieving command execution without any authentication token.
expected_security_impact: Sending a crafted POST request to /api/runscript with test mode enabled and code containing require('child_process').execSync('echo <proof_string>') should execute the echo command server-side and return the proof string in the HTTP response body, confirming arbitrary code execution.
actual_observed_result: Running the exploit locally against the FUXA v1.2.7 server: POST /api/runscript without any x-access-token header returned HTTP 200 with the proof string in the response body: "EVOHUNT_RCE_PROOF_<timestamp>\n". This confirms that Module._compile at msm.js:143 executed require('child_process').execSync() and returned the command output. No authentication was required. The Docker-based reproduction bundle was not executed due to infrastructure constraints (Docker image pull timeout), but the identical exploit code path was verified through direct local execution.
verification_method: The verify script sends the exploit request with a unique proof string (EVOHUNT_RCE_PROOF_<timestamp>), executes 'echo <proof_string>' via child_process.execSync through the script engine, and checks that the proof string appears in the HTTP response body. This distinguishes genuine command execution from error messages, startup failures, or unrelated responses.
evidence_produced_by_run: POST /api/runscript returned HTTP 200 with response body containing the proof string, confirming server-side code execution via Module._compile.
No x-access-token header was provided in the request, confirming the endpoint is accessible without credentials (verifyToken auto-generated a guest token and never blocked the request).
The response contained the output of the injected echo command, confirming execution in the Node.js server process context.
limitations_or_blockers: The Docker-based reproduction bundle (./reproduction/run.sh) was not executed due to Docker base image pull timeout in the test environment. The identical exploit was verified through local execution of the FUXA server.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the FUXA repository from GitHub, checks out the pinned vulnerable tag v1.2.7, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
